lines: Castle Scribe Elowen, at your service and only slightly buried in dust.
The library keeps the old seal-name; the armory keeps the banner that proves it. Bring me both truths before the dungeon stair.
Castle Scribe Elowen, at your service. Celia Knotwell's ribbon-word arrived on your boots before her cart could manage it.
If the Sunken Road is shedding stones, we shall answer with ink and thread: library seal-name, armory banner, then the dungeon stair.
A castle remembers in pairs: ink in the library, thread in the armory. One without the other is merely gossip.
The seal is remembered: sun over thorn, gold over ash. The stair will answer if you carry the key with a steady hand.
The seal is remembered, and I have written your name small but legible beside it.
